title: The last stage of a pipeline runs in a subshell, so its variable assignments vanish
rule: In bash (no `lastpipe`), every pipeline stage runs in its own subshell, so variables assigned inside `cmd | while read ...; do count=$((count+1)); done` are discarded when the pipeline ends and the parent keeps the old value. Keep the loop in the current shell instead: feed it via process substitution `while ...; done < <(cmd)` or a here-string `while ...; done <<<"$var"`. (`shopt -s lastpipe` also works but only in bash 4.2+ with job control off, e.g. `set +m`.)
triggers: counter is 0 after while read loop
variable not updated after pipe to while
array empty after building it in a piped loop
value reset outside while loop bash
